title: Bridge Essay: Brain Structural Correlates of Metacognition in First-Episode Psychosis.
concept: Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
**Phase context:** Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

**Brain Structural Correlates of Metacognition in First-Episode Psychosis.** — Schizophr Bull (2020). DOI: 10.1093/schbul/sbz116. PMID: 31776577. Metacognition is impaired in schizophrenia and is an important predictor of functional outcome, but the underlying neuropathology is not clear. Studies have implicated frontal regions and there is also some evidence that the hippocampus might play a pivotal role, but findings are inconsistent. We set out to more comprehensively investigate the neural underpinnings of insight in first-episode psychosis (FEP) using 2 metacognitive measures (the Beck Cognitive Insight Scale [BCIS]) and a perceptual
